data.title: Intel plans aggressive 2026 capital expenditure cuts to prioritize customer demand-driven investments, after 'excessive' past spending. CEO Lip-Bu Tan aims for financially disciplined approach, focusing on what customers need. CFO confirms commitment to reducing CapEx.
data.description: Intel Corp. (NASDAQ: INTC) is planning aggressive cuts in capital expenditures for 2026, with CEO Lip-Bu Tan emphasizing a shift towards demand-driven investments over past "unwise and excessive" spending. The company's second quarter 2025 earnings call highlighted this new financially disciplined strategy. Intel's CFO, David Zinsner, confirmed the commitment to reducing capital outlays. This change follows acknowledgment from Intel's CEO that past capacity investments were ahead of demand. The company aims to prioritize building what customers need, when they need it, to earn their trust through consistent execution. This strategy marks a significant departure from previous practices. CEO Lip-Bu Tan's statement on the philosophy for Intel's foundry business indicates a shift towards a more customer-centric approach. Intel's current stock price and further details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: ENS price jumps 3.09% to $27.71, signaling potential breakout above $30 resistance. Ethereum Name Service RSI is neutral.
data.description: The price of Ethereum Name Service (ENS) has surged to $27.71, showcasing bullish technical indicators that suggest a possible breakthrough above the $30 resistance level. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) for Ethereum Name Service is currently hovering in neutral territory, indicating a balanced market sentiment. This positive trend hints at a potential further rise in the ENS price, attracting investors looking for lucrative opportunities in the cryptocurrency market. Traders are closely monitoring the developments, anticipating a potential breakout beyond the $30 mark.

data.title: Samsung releases Galaxy Z Fold7 and Galaxy Z Flip7 along with Galaxy Watch8 globally. Galaxy Z Fold7 dominates pre-orders in Europe with over half targets exceeded and more than 50% increase in consumer interest compared to previous model.
data.description: Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. unveiled the worldwide release of its new foldable smartphones, the Galaxy Z Fold7 and Galaxy Z Flip7, along with the Galaxy Watch8 series. The Galaxy Z series has gained traction in Europe, surpassing pre-order expectations before the deadline. The Galaxy Z Fold7, the flagship model, comprises more than 50% of pre-orders and has experienced a 50% surge in pre-orders from European customers compared to previous figures.

data.title: Asyad Express launches tech-driven reverse logistics platform for e-commerce parcel returns in GCC, improving customer experience. (Total characters: 131)
data.description: Asyad Express in Oman has launched a new service to further establish itself as a reliable e-commerce logistics partner. The service aims to improve the customer experience by providing quick and efficient return solutions connecting Oman and major GCC locations. The announcement was made in Muscat on July 25, 2025, showcasing Asyad's commitment to enhancing logistics operations.

data.title: Bandai Namco announces new Gundam projects at San Diego Comic-Con 2025. Major reveals for 3 series.
data.description: Today at the opening night of San Diego Comic-Con 2025, Bandai Namco Filmworks Inc. made significant announcements regarding three key Gundam series during the panel event "GUNDAM...". This event took place in Los Angeles on July 25, 2025, attracting a large audience as it kicked off one of the biggest pop culture and entertainment conventions worldwide. The news unveiled during this event is expected to impact the entertainment industry and fans worldwide, setting the tone for the convention and generating buzz among attendees.

data.title: L3Harris ready to deploy 40-45 Hypersonic Ballistic Tracking Space Sensor satellites for Trump's 'Golden Dome' missile defense.
data.description: L3Harris Technologies Inc., a major defense contractor listed on NYSE as LHX, has announced its readiness to meet the demands of President Donald Trump's "Golden Dome" missile defense initiative. CEO Christopher Kubasik revealed during the second-quarter earnings call that the company is gearing up to deploy a complete set of 40 to 45 proven HPTSS satellites promptly. These satellites, known as Hypersonic Ballistic Tracking Space Sensor satellites, are a crucial element of the Golden Dome missile defense system. Kubasik emphasized the significance of this effort, labeling it as one of the most significant homeland security undertakings in the nation's history. The initiative is tied to a $175 billion plan unveiled by Trump in May. For more details, visit the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Filecoin (FIL) price at $2.56 falls, struggles below moving averages despite updates. Foundation releases have neutral market impact.
data.description: The FIL price is currently at $2.56, a decrease from its recent peak levels. Technical indicators are showing conflicting signals. Foundation releases have had a neutral impact on the market, coinciding with a downward trend.
